# Customer quote templates

Customer quote templates control how your quote looks to the customer. Logo, header text, contact details, footer disclaimers. The line items come from the quote itself - the template is the wrapper.

## When to use them

- You want different branding for different audiences (residential vs commercial).
- You want different terms or disclaimers for different job types.
- You want a fast-loaded layout instead of editing the header and footer per quote.

You can have as many templates as you want and pick one per quote.

## Steps to create one

1. Go to **Templates** in the workspace nav. Find the customer quote templates section.
2. Click **New Customer Quote Template**.
3. Add a name.
4. Set the header content - your logo, company name, contact details, anything you want at the top of every quote that uses this template.
5. Set the footer - terms and conditions, payment notes, validity period, disclaimers.
6. Save.

## Steps to use one

1. Open a quote.
2. Click **Customer Quote** from the summary page.
3. In the editor, pick your template from the dropdown.
4. The header and footer fill in. Edit the line items as normal.

## What's in the template

- Logo (or use your company default).
- Header text - usually company name, address, phone, email.
- Customer info layout (this is mostly fixed, but you can change formatting).
- Footer text - terms, validity, payment expectations.
- Quote number formatting.

## What's not

- The line items - those come from the quote itself.
- The customer's name and details - those come from the quote.
- Pricing - that comes from the quote.

The template is the frame around the data, not the data.

## Tips

- Mark one as default. New customer quotes pick it up automatically.
- If you change a template, existing quotes that used it don't update - they keep the version they were created with.
- Use the footer for anything you want every customer to see: validity period, deposit terms, scope exclusions.
